ab: O fakeloh ko sapitalakal to ’edo a mi’enec, roma i, o wa’il ko sapitalakal to tolok a milamec, matatodongay haenen.
ch: A:老鼠應該要⽤⽯壓式陷阱抓，雉雞應該要⽤套頸式陷阱抓，這樣才對。
en: It is correct to catch mice through rangay and catch bamboo partridges through qjiulang.

ab: Mahaenay han, mararaway ko demak ako! salaw awa ko ma’adpay no mako.
ch: B:原來是這樣，我都搞錯了！難怪我都抓不到。
en: No wonder! I am mistaken and that’s why I cannot catch any of them.

ab: Liyawen haca ako pakafana’ kiso misanga’ to talakal.
ch: A:那我再教你怎麼製作這兩種陷阱。
en: Then I will teach you how to set up these two traps.

ab: wata! kahemekan to! Ano miliyaw mi’adop haenen to ako a mitalakal nga’ citama’.
ch: B:好啊！太棒了!這樣我以後就會抓到獵物了。
en: Great! I will be able to catch the prey in the future.

ab: Alaen ko kawkaw ikofaw, tayra kita i omah nifaliw to semot, ’akawangay to ko semot iomah.
ch: A:去倉庫拿鐮刀，我們去田裡砍草，田裡的草太高了。
en: Go to the warehouse to get the sickle, we go to the field to cut the grass, the grass in the field is too high.

ab: I cowaan miteli ko kawkaw?
ch: B:鐮刀放在哪裡?
en: Where are the sickles?

ab: Itiraay ifati’ian no tanomal, comod sa kiso ma’araw to.
ch: A:在圓簍的旁邊，你進去倉庫就會看到了。
en: It is next to the round basket. You will see it when you enter the warehouse.

ab: Na’onen miala, cihinangay ko kawkaw.
ch: A:小心拿，鐮刀很利。
en: Be careful and don’t get hurt. The sickle is very sharp.

ab: Aka ripa’en ko talakal ako!
ch: A:不要踩到我的陷阱!
en: Don’t step on my trap!

ab: I cowaan iso miteli ko talakal?
ch: B:你的陷阱在哪裡?
en: Where is your trap?

ab: I laeno no kilang a telien no mako ko talakal.
ch: A:我的陷阱在那棵樹的下面。
en: My trap is under that tree.

ab: Mana cakatawal iso?
ch: B:你怎麼會記得?
en: How do you remember?

ab: Ira itira ko nikonisan ako, mafana’ kako to icowaan ako miteli ko talakal.
ch: A:我在這邊有做記號，我知道我的陷阱在哪裡。
en: I make a mark here, so I know where my trap is.

ab: O foefo koni, telien no mita i lawac no ’alo a mifoting.
ch: A:這是魚筌，我們放在溪邊捕魚。
en: This is a fish trap which we put beside the river for fishing.

ab: Awaay itira ko foefo niyam, o pacemot ko sapifoting niyam.
ch: B:我們那邊沒有魚筌，我們都用釣竿抓魚。
en: We don’t have any fish traps there, so we catch fish with a fishing rod.

ab: Caho pitanam kako a mipacemot, anocila pakafana’ han to kako?
ch: A:我沒有用過釣竿，你下次教我好嗎?
en: I have never used a fishing rod before, would you mind teaching me next time?

ab: Hai, anocila malacafay kita a mipacemot.
ch: B:好啊!我們下次一起去釣魚。
en: Sure! Let’s go fishing together next time.

ab: Liyaw to a romi’ad pakafana’ han to ako misanga’ to foefoe kiso.
ch: A:那我改天教你做魚筌。
en: I will teach you how to make a fish trap next time.

ab: Tatemikay to ko pacemot iso, mapacemotay to ko foting alatek, halikien a mitengteng.
ch: A:你的釣竿動了，是不是有魚上鉤了!你快拉起來。
en: Your fishing rod is moving. Isn’t the fish on the hook? Pull it up!

ab: Aka ho, ngiyangi sanay ko tamina. Itini sa kako mitengteng to pacemot, patadoen ho kako tayra miala to salil.
ch: B:等等，船太搖動了。我在這邊拉釣竿，去那邊幫我拿網子來。
en: Wait, the ship is rolling heavily. I am here to pull the fishing rod and you should go there to grab me the net.

ab: Tengtengen patayni kora foting, patadoen no mako kiso a mitengteng.
ch: A:你把魚拉到我這邊。我幫妳拉起來。
en: Pull the fish to my side and I will help you pull it up.

ab: Wata! ka kadofa anini, matomes ko kopid.
ch: A:太好了!今天大豐收，魚簍都裝滿了。
en: Great! It is a bumper crop today with full fish baskets.

ab: Halikien a minokay papalilamen no mita ko salawinawina.
ch: B:我們快回去跟親戚分享。
en: Let’s go back immediately and share it with everyone.
